title: Internal Medicine — Mixed PMH and HPI
note:
Chief Complaint:
Chest pain and shortness of breath for 2 days.

History of Present Illness:
58-year-old male presenting with acute onset chest pain since yesterday morning.
He also reports worsening dyspnea. Denies fever or cough.

Past Medical History:
Hypertension diagnosed 8 years ago. History of pneumonia (resolved 2 years ago).
Type 2 diabetes mellitus, well-controlled on metformin.

Assessment:
Possible angina. Rule out pulmonary embolism.
Hypertension stable. Diabetes controlled.

title: Pulmonology — Negation-heavy ROS
note:
Review of Systems:
Positive for fatigue and headache. Negative for chest pain, fever, nausea, or vomiting.
No shortness of breath at rest.

Assessment:
Migraine, ongoing. Patient denies any history of seizures.
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, stable.

title: Cardiology Follow-up — Pronoun Coreference
note:
History of Present Illness:
Patient was admitted last month for atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response.
It was successfully cardioverted. She now reports palpitations returning.

Past Medical History:
Coronary artery disease. Deep vein thrombosis (prior episode, resolved).
Hyperlipidemia managed with statins.

Assessment:
Atrial fibrillation recurrence. Possible heart failure exacerbation.

title: Discharge Summary — Temporal Signals
note:
History of Present Illness:
Patient presents with acute onset shortness of breath this morning.
She had pneumonia 6 months ago which fully resolved.
Currently no chest pain. Fever is absent.

Past Medical History:
Rheumatoid arthritis in remission. Osteoporosis diagnosed 3 years ago.
Prior MI five years ago.

Assessment:
Suspected pulmonary embolism. Anxiety ruled out.

title: Inpatient Admission — HPI Resolution (trajectory: resolution)
note:
History of Present Illness:
65-year-old patient presenting with pneumonia, confirmed on chest X-ray.
Pneumonia was treated with IV antibiotics for 5 days.
Pneumonia resolved by discharge.
Fever was present on admission.
Fever resolved with antipyretics within 48 hours.
Cough was noted at admission.
Cough resolved by day 4.

Past Medical History:
Hypertension, well-controlled on lisinopril.
Type 2 diabetes mellitus, stable on metformin.

title: PMH Relapse Note — Trajectory: relapse (resolved → ongoing)
note:
Past Medical History:
History of depression. Depression is currently relapsing with worsening mood.
History of atrial fibrillation. Atrial fibrillation has recurred and is ongoing.
Hypertension, under control.

History of Present Illness:
Patient presents with chest pain.
Shortness of breath is currently worsening.

expected.note: Trajectory relapse: history of → currently relapsing
Trajectory relapse: history of → recurred and ongoing
PMH prior override (under control = low-confidence → section prior)

title: HPI Clarification Note — Trajectory: clarification (ambiguous → definite)
note:
History of Present Illness:
Possible sepsis on initial presentation. Sepsis is currently active with positive blood cultures.
Suspected pneumonia on chest X-ray. Pneumonia is ongoing and confirmed on CT.
Cannot rule out pulmonary embolism. Pulmonary embolism has been ruled out on CT angiography.

Past Medical History:
Hypertension, well-controlled.
